note: The combined JS2060 admission entry maps to two independently published 2025-2026 Handbook curricula. Music publishes 37 units of Major Required Courses, one 21-unit concentration and two required 3-unit Honours Project courses. Its page labels Major Courses as 69 units, but the explicit structure is 37 + 21 = 58 units, and 58 Major + 6 Honours Project + 9 University Language + 22 General Education + 33 Free Electives closes the stated 128-unit degree; the conflicting 69-unit label is retained as a manual-review issue rather than used to invent courses. Music offers Composition, Directed Studies, Music Education and Performance concentrations. Directed Studies is an open coordinator-directed 21-unit pool: the displayed courses are recorded, but the list is not presented as closed, and the official 6-unit cap on Chamber Music / Group Instrument / Vocal Study remains applicable. The Music Education page labels the concentration 21 units, but its displayed Chamber Music path contains six 1-unit courses while the other fixed and choose-one requirements add 17 units, producing 23; this conflict requires manual review. The Performance table repeats MUSI4007, MUSI4015, MUSI4038 and MUSI4039; each code is stored once with all published roles, and either displayed path can reconcile to 21 units after duplicate codes are counted once. Creative Industries publishes 22 units of Major Required Courses, 15 units selected from an explicit 82-course Major Elective pool, one 21-unit concentration, and two required 3-unit Honours Project courses, closing 58 Major units plus 6 Honours Project units. It offers Popular Music Performance and Songwriting and Scoring for Film, Television and Video Games concentrations. MUSI2057 is assigned 2 units by both curricula while the individual course page assigns 3 units; the curriculum-specific 2-unit value is retained and flagged. MUSI3055 has no unit value in the Directed Studies table; its individual course page supplies 3 units. MUSI4025 and MUSI4026 use stale 2024-2025 links on the current Creative Industries page, while the current 2025-2026 course-directory pages confirm their titles and 2-unit values. Courses shared across the two curricula are duplicated only across their separate Major supplements, preserving Major-specific roles and local identity. University Language, General Education and Free Electives are excluded from both supplements. No Year or Term is assigned because the official requirement tables do not publish course timing. Concentration evidence is taken from the six official child pages under the two supplement source URLs.
